title: Rosa glauca var. obovata
what_description: Rosa glauca var. obovata is a botanical variety of the species Rosa glauca, belonging to the family Rosaceae. This evergreen shrub is characterized by its ovate to obovate leaves, typically 10-20 cm long and 5-10 cm wide, with a glaucous indumentum on the lower surface. The leaves are pinnate with 3-5 leaflets, and the flowers are pink to red, fragrant, and borne in terminal corymbose inflorescences. Each flower has 5-7 petals and a calyx with 5 sepals. The variety Rosa glauca var. obovata is endemic to the Mediterranean region.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rosa glauca var. obovata are inflammation, which is reduced due to its anti-inflammatory properties, skin irritation, caused by its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, and digestive issues, alleviated by its prebiotic and anti-inflammatory effects. Rosa glauca var. obovata also helps in managing diabetes through its antidiabetic properties, while its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties aid in reducing arthritis. Additionally, it has been found to prevent diarrhea by regulating gut bacteria, and promote wound healing due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also aid in preventing skin conditions, such as eczema, and wound healing. Furthermore, Rosa glauca var. obovata has been found to prevent cancer by reducing oxidative stress, and its antimicrobial properties also prevent fungal infections, fever, allergies, hypertension, infections, stress, respiratory problems, ulcers, and high blood pressure.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rosa glauca var. obovata are its antioxidant activity, which involves the neutralization of free radicals, thereby preventing oxidative stress and cell damage. Antimicrobial properties of the plant, attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids, have been demonstrated in various studies, showing its potential in combating microbial infections. Its anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of saponins and flavonoids, may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The analgesic properties of Rosa glauca var. obovata have been linked to the presence of volatile oils and flavonoids, which may provide relief from pain and discomfort. Furthermore, its cardioprotective properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids, may help protect against cardiovascular diseases and improve cardiovascular health.

constituents.name: rosa glauca var. obovata phenolic acids

constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rosa glauca var. obovata are rosmarinic acid, quercetin, gallic acid, kaempferol, and ellagic acid. These phenolic compounds exhibit potent antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to the plant's therapeutic potential. Rosmarinic acid has been shown to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, while quercetin has been found to scavenge free radicals and chelate metals, thereby protecting against oxidative stress. Gallic acid has been demonstrated to possess antimicrobial activity against a range of pathogens, including bacteria, viruses, and fungi, while kaempferol has been found to inhibit cancer cell proliferation and induce apoptosis. Ellagic acid, a polyphenolic compound, has been shown to exhibit anti-carcinogenic and anti-inflammatory activity, and has been found to inhibit the growth of various types of cancer cells.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rosa glauca var. obovata include tea, made by infusing the leaves or flowers in hot water, which is traditionally used for its astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. Tincture, a concentrated liquid extract, can be prepared by steeping the plant material in a solvent like ethanol or glycerin, often used for its antioxidant and antimicrobial effects. Salve, a topical cream, can be made by infusing the plant material in a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and then mixing it with beeswax, often used for its anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing properties. Infusion, a liquid made by steeping the plant material in hot water, is also used to make a medicinal tea, while infused oil, a liquid made by steeping the plant material in a carrier oil, can be used topically for its anti-inflammatory and skin-soothing effects. These preparations are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin irritations, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rosa glauca var. obovata improperly include inducing nausea, which can be caused by its iridoid glycosides and anthocyanins that may irritate the stomach and intestines. Additionally, using Rosa glauca var. obovata can also cause allergic reactions, such as redness, itching, and swelling, in some individuals due to its potential allergenic properties. In rare cases, it may exacerbate diarrhea in people with sensitive digestive systems, as its anthocyanins and other compounds can alter gut bacteria and intestinal permeability. Furthermore, its allergenic properties may also induce allergic reactions in individuals with pre-existing allergies or sensitivities. It is essential to use Rosa glauca var. obovata under the guidance of a healthcare professional to minimize the risk of these side effects.
